overview: This five-color Pioneer deck leverages Mox Amber to fetch key lands and Beseech the Mirror to duplicate permanents, culminating in a Song of Creation payoff. By rapidly assembling a board of 1/1 creatures, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize. The strategy relies on precise sequencing and mana acceleration to bypass early-game vulnerability.
win_conditions: Casting Song of Creation to create a 1/1 creature for each land controlled
Using Mox Amber to fetch lands for Beseech the Mirror
Copying key permanents with Beseech the Mirror to apply pressure

keep_criteria: Hands containing Mox Amber and early lands
Hands with Beseech the Mirror and mana sources
Hands featuring Song of Creation and acceleration
Keep any hand with Mox Amber or Beseech the Mirror plus two lands.
Keep Song of Creation only if paired with fast mana (Mox Amber, fetches, or shock lands) and a land.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only Song of Creation and no mana, or hands with Beseech but no way to get Song of Creation to the graveyard.

early_game: Prioritize playing lands and Mox Amber
Search for specific lands needed for combo pieces
Protect combo pieces from early disruption
Turn 1: Play a fetch land, tap for a basic, fetch a dual/shock land, and play Mox Amber.
If it is countered or removed, use Beseech the Mirror to sacrifice a permanent and recur Song of Creation from the graveyard.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast Song of Creation on time while retaining mana for protection or recursion.
mid_game: Cast Beseech the Mirror to copy key permanents
Accumulate mana and creatures for the payoff
Apply pressure with copied creatures or lands
Prioritize keeping Song of Creation and Beseech the Mirror in hand or graveyard.
Use Mox Amber for tempo and mana acceleration, timing your spells to trigger its untap ability.
keep recursion tools ready and manage your graveyard carefully if facing hate cards.
Commit threats only when you have protection or recursion backup.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or heavy removal.
late_game: Cast Song of Creation to flood the board
Use the army of 1/1s to attack directly
Maintain board presence with copied permanents
Song of Creation is the primary finisher.
Beseech the Mirror enables recursion wins if you have multiple copies or ways to sacrifice permanents.
Use Mox Amber to fuel the recursion loop and ensure you can cast Song of Creation even after it's been answered.
Go all-in when you have a clear path to resolving Song of Creation, have answered or bypassed the opponent's removal, or when you're ahead on board and can protect the win condition.
Commit fully if the opponent has no interaction left.

key_cards.name: Song of Creation
key_cards.role: Payoff spell that creates a 1/1 creature for each land you control
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have maximum lands and mana to overwhelm the board quickly

key_cards.name: Mox Amber
key_cards.role: Artifact that fetches a land of any type
key_cards.notes: Use to find specific lands needed for Beseech the Mirror or to boost land count for Song of Creation

key_cards.name: Beseech the Mirror
key_cards.role: Sorcery that copies a target permanent
key_cards.notes: Copy lands to increase land count or copy creatures to apply early pressure

sequencing_tips: Always play Mox Amber before casting high-cost spells
Copy lands with Beseech the Mirror before casting Song of Creation
Hold up mana for potential counterspells or removal
Common mistake: Casting Song of Creation without a plan for recursion if it's answered.
Always keep Beseech the Mirror in hand or ensure you have a way to get it to the graveyard.
Watch out for cantrips that let opponents find removal.
Mox Amber's untap trigger can be a massive tempo swing, so time your spells to maximize it.
The deck is fragile to mass removal, so don't overcommit to the board before casting Song of Creation.

matchups.advice: Prioritize copying creatures and playing lands quickly to stabilize
Rush the combo before they can interact with your spells
Use copied permanents to apply pressure while setting up the payoff

Use split second if available, or cast Song of Creation when the opponent has no mana up. Use Beseech the Mirror to recur it after it's countered. Employ cheap threats to force interaction, then win with Song of Creation on the following turn.

Apply early pressure with Mox Amber and cheap spells. Use spot removal to stop key ramp pieces. Don't let them get too far ahead; recur Song of Creation quickly and pressure their life total to close the game before they stabilize.

Win the recursion war. Prioritize card advantage and protection. Use Beseech the Mirror to recur Song of Creation faster. Sideboard in extra recursion and hate for their recursion tools. Trade efficiently and force them to overcommit to removal.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Damnation), targeted removal (Path, Swords to Plowshares), and graveyard hate (Leyline, Rest in Peace).
Note if opponents are holding up removal for Song of Creation versus early threats, and adjust your recursion timing accordingly.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Song of Creation, Mox Amber, Beseech the Mirror
